note.soap:
S:  
No history or symptoms provided. No allergies reported. No additional clinical information available from the patient.

O:  
No physical exam findings or vital signs available due to phone-only encounter.

A:  
  - Working diagnosis (most likely):  
    Unable to determine working diagnosis due to lack of clinical information.  

  - Differential:  
    Cannot be established without symptom data.  

  - Red flags considered:  
    None identified due to absence of reported symptoms or history.

P:  
  - Disposition:  
    Recommend patient call back or seek in-person evaluation if symptoms develop or worsen.  

  - Plan:  
    Await further history or symptom report to guide diagnosis and management.  

  - Safety net:  
    Advise patient to seek urgent care or emergency services if experiencing severe symptoms such as difficulty breathing, chest pain, sudden weakness, high fever, or altered mental status.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.
